system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoCentralFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         4;

deltaT          0.002;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   0.1;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;

adjustTimeStep  no;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

fluxScheme      Kurganov;

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(tauMC)      Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
    reconstruct(rho) vanLeer;
    reconstruct(U)  vanLeerV;
    reconstruct(T)  vanLeer;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "(rho|rhoU|rhoE)"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        nSweeps         2;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    e
    {
        $U;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  laminar;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
    energy          sensibleInternalEnergy;
}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   28.96;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          1004.5;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          0;
        Pr          1;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Vertices for single hex block covering full domain
    // Bottom face (z = -0.05)
    (-1 -1 -0.05)   // 0
    (3 -1 -0.05)    // 1
    (3 -0.2 -0.05)  // 2
    (-1 -0.2 -0.05) // 3

    // Top face (z = +0.05)
    (-1 -1 0.05)    // 4
    (3 -1 0.05)     // 5
    (3 -0.2 0.05)   // 6
    (-1 -0.2 0.05)  // 7

    // Additional vertices for top face y = 0.2
    (-1 0.2 -0.05)  // 8
    (3 0.2 -0.05)   // 9
    (3 0.2 0.05)    // 10
    (-1 0.2 0.05)   // 11
);

blocks
(
    // Single hex block covering the full domain
    // Bottom face: vertices 0,1,2,3 at y=-1 to y=-0.2
    // Top face: vertices 8,9,10,11 at y=0.2
    // But we need a valid hex - redefine properly
    // Using vertices 0,1,9,8 as bottom face (y=-1 to y=0.2, z=-0.05)
    // and vertices 4,5,10,11 as top face (y=-1 to y=0.2, z=+0.05)
    // This gives us a valid hex with correct winding
    hex (0 1 9 8 4 5 10 11) (75 25 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 8 11 4)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (1 9 10 5)
        );
    }
    bottom
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 8)
        );
    }
    top
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (8 9 10 11)
        );
    }
    obstacle
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (3 2 6 7)
            (3 8 11 7)
        );
    }
    defaultFaces
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='T', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      T;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 0 0 1 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    top
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    obstacle
    {
        type            slip;
    }

    defaultFaces
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volVectorField;
    object      U;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 1 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform (1.3 0 0);

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform (1.3 0 0);
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            inletOutlet;
        inletValue      uniform (1.3 0 0);
        value           uniform (1.3 0 0);
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    top
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    obstacle
    {
        type            slip;
    }

    defaultFaces
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      p;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [1 -1 -2 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            waveTransmissive;
        field           p;
        psi             thermo:psi;
        gamma           1.4;
        fieldInf        1;
        lInf            1;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    top
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    obstacle
    {
        type            slip;
    }

    defaultFaces
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //')]</foamfiles>

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Understanding the Current State

After 8 attempts, the `blockMesh` error persists. The error has consistently been about boundary faces not being valid external faces of their blocks, or blocks being inside-out/degenerate. Previous attempts have tried various fixes — removing faces from defaultFaces, correcting face definitions, restructuring blocks — but the fundamental problem is that the **multi-block L-shaped topology combined with these specific vertex coordinates creates geometrically invalid hexahedra**.

Looking at the **current** error:

```
face 0 in patch 2 does not have neighbour cell face: 4(0 3 12 9)
```

This face `(0 3 12 9)` is listed in `bottom` but is NOT a valid face of Block 0. Despite previous analyses suggesting it might be, the error definitively proves it isn't. The issue is that the vertex positions and block connectivity create blocks where certain faces don't align with the hex topology conventions.

## New Approach: Adopt the Exact ForwardStep Tutorial blockMeshDict

Given that 8 attempts of custom modifications have failed, the only remaining viable option is to **use the exact blockMeshDict from the official OpenFOAM forwardStep tutorial**. The tutorial provides a proven, working 4-block L-shaped mesh topology that satisfies all blockMesh requirements.

### Steps to Fix:

1. **Replace the entire `blockMeshDict`** with the exact content from `foam/tutorial/compressible/rhoCentralFoam/forwardStep/system/blockMeshDict`. This is the reference case mentioned in the similar case reference.

2. **Only modify the tutorial's blockMeshDict minimally** to match user requirements:
   - **Scale vertex coordinates** appropriately to fit the user's domain (keep step proportions)
   - **Adjust cell counts** in each block to achieve roughly deltaT=0.002 resolution
   - **Rename boundary patches**:
     - `left` → `inlet`
     - `right` → `outlet`
     - Keep step faces as `obstacle`
     - Keep `bottom` and `top` as `symmetryPlane`
     - Keep internal shared faces as `defaultFaces`
   - **Ensure all boundary faces** in the `bottom` patch are actual external faces of single blocks (the tutorial's faces are already correct)

3. **Update the `0/` field files** (`T`, `U`, `p`) to include the `obstacle` boundary condition with `type slip;` (already done in the current files — verify they match the new blockMeshDict patch names).

4. **Keep all other files unchanged** (`controlDict`, `fvSchemes`, `fvSolution`, `momentumTransport`, `physicalProperties`).

### Why This Will Work

The forwardStep tutorial's blockMeshDict has been validated countless times. Its 4-block topology with proper vertex sharing and correct face definitions guarantees that:
- Every block is a proper convex hexahedron (no inside-out/degenerate blocks)
- Every boundary face is a valid external face of exactly one block
- Every internal face is shared by exactly two blocks (listed in `defaultFaces`)
- The `bottom` patch faces are all genuine external boundary faces

The previous 8 attempts failed because custom block topologies were forced onto vertex positions that didn't naturally form valid hexahedra for that connectivity. The tutorial solves this by providing a geometrically sound topology where the vertex positions and block connectivity are mutually compatible.

## Error 2: `log.rhoCentralFoam` — Cannot find "points"

This is a cascading error. Once `blockMesh` succeeds, the mesh will be generated and this error will resolve automatically.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Replace entire blockMeshDict with the standard forwardStep tutorial blockMeshDict content; Redefine vertices for a proper L-shaped multi-block topology: vertex 0 = (-1,-1,-0.05), vertex 1 = (0,-1,-0.05), vertex 2 = (0,-0.2,-0.05), vertex 3 = (-1,-0.2,-0.05), vertex 4 = (-1,-1,0.05), vertex 5 = (0,-1,0.05), vertex 6 = (0,-0.2,0.05), vertex 7 = (-1,-0.2,0.05), vertex 8 = (0,0.2,-0.05), vertex 9 = (3,0.2,-0.05), vertex 10 = (3,-0.2,-0.05), vertex 11 = (0,-0.2,0.05) -- actually keep existing vertices but restructure; Use 4 hex blocks forming an L-shape around the step corner at origin: block0 hex(0 1 5 4 3 7 11 8) for region x:-1..0 y:-1..-0.2 z:-0.05..0.05, block1 hex(1 2 6 5 4 8 10 11) for region x:0..3 y:-1..-0.2 z:-0.05..0.05, block2 hex(0 4 7 3 8 12 15 13) for region x:-1..0 y:-0.2..0.2 z:-0.05..0.05, block3 hex(1 5 9 2 4 8 13 14) for region x:0..3 y:-0.2..0.2 z:-0.05..0.05 -- wait, need to carefully define 4 proper hex blocks; Actually adopt the standard tutorial approach: 6 vertices on each side of the step; Define 4 blocks around the step corner; Set cell counts per block to approximate deltaT=0.002 resolution over total domain size; Use cell counts (25 1 1) (75 1 1) (25 1 1) (75 1 1) for the four blocks respectively; boundary patches: inlet = left face of block0, outlet = right face of block1 and block3, bottom = bottom face of block0 and block1, top = top face of block0 and block1, obstacle = step-top and step-front and step-back faces, defaultFaces = all internal shared faces between blocks'}, {'file': '0/T', 'changes': 'Add obstacle boundary entry if not present with type slip; Already has it so no change needed'}, {'file': '0/U', 'changes': 'Add obstacle boundary entry if not present with type slip; Already has it so no change needed'}, {'file': '0/p', 'changes': 'Add obstacle boundary entry if not present with type slip; Already has it so no change needed'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a laminar, compressible flow over a forward-facing step using the rhoCentralFoam solver. Boundary conditions include a fixed velocity of 1.3m/s and temperature of 1 K at the inlet and slip conditions on the obstacle. Use timestep of 0.002 and output every 0.1. Finaltime is 4.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
